steps.title: Identify the two curves on the graph
steps.content: The graph contains two curves: a straight line (the linear equation) and a parabola opening upward (the nonlinear equation). The solution to the system is the point where these two curves intersect.

steps.title: Locate the intersection and read its coordinates
steps.content: Visually trace where the line and parabola meet. At the intersection point, the $x$-coordinate is $-2$ and the $y$-coordinate is $6$. The solution is $(-2,6)$, which matches choice B.
